headline: Market overview Day 1
detail:
1) Benchmark indices spent the day in a largely unchanged tone, with the average move across large-cap stocks around 0.09% and clear leadership emerging from the Infra space.
2) Banking names such as HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK and SBIN lagged the broader move as traders booked partial profits, as participants reacted to liquidity conditions, deposit trends and evolving rate expectations.
3) Infra stood out as the strongest performing sector with an average change of 1.44%, while Cement underperformed with a softer move of -1.65%, highlighting ongoing rotation within the market.
4) Domestic flows in mutual funds and systematic investment plans continued to provide a steady bid to quality large caps, even as global cues around US yields, crude prices and geopolitical headlines kept traders selectively active.
5) Overall sentiment remained balanced, with investors preferring fundamentally strong Indian companies, accumulating on intraday dips while keeping an eye on upcoming macro data, RBI communication and sector-specific policy developments.
